evals.prompt: We just finished adding the billing settings page and a reusable InvoiceStatusBadge component. Run syncdocs so the context files tell the truth.
evals.expected_output: Scopes likely drift from the completed feature, compares implementation facts to existing context files, updates only relevant context/*.md files, and reports files updated plus unresolved drift.
expectations: Scopes the analysis by checking recent work and progress-tracker before broad reading
Looks for drift in progress-tracker, ui-registry, architecture, code-standards, library-docs, build-plan, ui-tokens, and ui-rules as relevant
Updates only context/*.md files and never changes source code or tests
Does not pre-document features that do not exist
Preserves existing context-file structure and conventions
Reports files updated, drift detected but not fixed, and an overall verdict

evals.prompt: Run syncdocs only for context/progress-tracker.md. A feature that used to be listed as next is now complete; don't touch architecture or UI docs.
evals.expected_output: Honors the requested narrow scope, checks whether progress-tracker status and completed/pending work match reality, updates only progress-tracker.md, and reports any uncertainty rather than editing unrelated files.
expectations: Honors the user-specified scope and does not default to all context files
Checks current status, completed features, next work, and stale decisions in progress-tracker.md
Updates only context/progress-tracker.md when changes are justified
Does not modify architecture, UI registry, UI token/rule, library, or source files
Leaves uncertain claims in drift detected but not fixed instead of inventing facts
Reports a concise Context Sync Report
